description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a raised support structure. The solar panels are dark, grid-patterned, and slightly elevated above a broad, flat base that appears to house electrical and mechanical components. The base includes several colored panels and enclosures, likely for inverters, batteries, or control units, with some areas highlighted in red and blue. The support structure beneath the solar array is composed of angled struts, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight capture. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments such as parks, parking lots, or community spaces. The materials and construction indicate durability and weather resistance, with a focus on efficient solar energy collection and conversion.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a raised, grid-patterned photovoltaic surface and a broad base housing electrical components. The structure is supported by angled struts and features colored access panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the solar panel array above the base, with a prominent red panel on the right side of the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the base, the angled support struts, and the full span of the solar panel array.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the grid of solar panels and multiple colored panels on the base, including a large blue section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended base, support structure, and additional red and blue panels beneath the solar array.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum support struts
attribute_array_configuration: single row of approximately 8 large panels arranged side by side
